page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. This KVM-hypervised instance features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, translating to 8.0 GB of memory per core. A key hardware highlight is the inclusion of 1919 GB of local SSD storage, bundled alongside a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The processor includes 33 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA instruction sets, though it lacks newer extensions like AVX512_VNNI or AMX.

Benchmark results indicate a performance profile that leans toward the lower tiers for compute-heavy tasks. The instance registered weak results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as in Geekbench single-core and multi-core benchmarks, placing it in the bottom 25%. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression was poor, landing in the bottom 10%. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks performed at an average level. Database-related operations showed mixed results; while Passmark database operations were weak, Redis SET operations achieved an average rating of 6.02 million operations per second. LLM inference performance was average for a small 135M model but dropped to poor for a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.i2ne.2xlarge offers a high density of local SSD storage and a generous memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a viable option for workloads that require substantial local data caching or medium-sized database deployments without demanding top-tier CPU compute speeds. The primary tradeoff is the modest processing power relative to the memory and storage allocation. This server is best suited for workloads such as localized databases, caching layers, Redis deployments, and general-purpose applications requiring high-capacity local storage rather than intensive computational or cryptographic processing.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 1919 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate a weak compute profile, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores placing in the bottom 25%, and single-threaded gzip compression in the bottom 10%. However, it maintains average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations, which reach 6.02 million operations per second. Given its high storage capacity and 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for storage-centric workloads. It is best suited for local databases, caching layers, and applications requiring significant local SSD capacity rather than intensive CPU processing.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1919 GB local SSD. It is suited for storage-heavy workloads despite modest CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.2xlarge features 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 1919 GB local SSD storage, optimized for storage-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A storage-dense virtual server featuring high-capacity local SSD and balanced memory, optimized for caching and localized database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs and 64 GB of system memory
1919 GB of bundled local SSD storage for high-capacity data caching
Average performance in Redis SET operations and large-block memory bandwidth
Weaker CPU compute and cryptographic performance relative to modern standards
Well-suited for localized databases, caching layers, and storage-centric applications
